intro: Camellia tenuiflora, also known as the Sasame or Ten-fu, is a species of flowering plant in the tea family (Theaceae) that is native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Camellia tenuiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Camellia tenuiflora is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation, and is also used to make a type of folk medicine called "Ten-fu" tea.
